description: Finding Card UX Layer 1: Redesign ArtifactCard and ArtifactDetail to prioritize finding hierarchy (severity/type, title, content, status, agent attribution last).
steps: 1. Redesign ArtifactCard.tsx with finding-first hierarchy: severity/type badge at top, title prominent, content preview, status secondary, agent attribution last.
2. Add severity visual treatment based on artifact type: risk=high (red), decision/recommendation=medium (amber), assumption/tradeoff/open-question=low (green/calm).
3. Update type labels to match: decision=Finding, risk=Risk, assumption=Assumption, tradeoff=Tradeoff, open-question=Question, recommendation=Fix.
4. Make status control secondary (smaller, right-aligned, does not dominate).
6. Redesign ArtifactDetail.tsx to match new hierarchy: title first, type/severity/status metadata, main content, optional reasoning/agent attribution, status control at bottom.
7. Handle missing/empty data gracefully without fake placeholders.
8. Preserve keyboard accessibility, mobile layout, aria labels.
acceptance_criteria: ArtifactCard shows severity/type badge prominently at top of card
Card hierarchy is: severity+type -> title -> content preview -> status -> agent attribution
Severity visual treatment: risk type gets red/high treatment, decision/recommendation get amber/medium, others get calm/low
Status dropdown still works with aria-label 'Change finding status'
Agent attribution is present but visually de-emphasized
ArtifactDetail shows title first, metadata second, content third, attribution and version last
Missing title/content handled gracefully (no fake placeholders)
verification: npm run build exits with code 0
npm run lint exits with code 0
findings: Both ArtifactCard and ArtifactDetail redesigned with finding-first hierarchy. Build passes (0 errors), lint passes (0 errors, 20 pre-existing warnings). Severity is derived from artifact.type since ArtifactState has no explicit severity field. No new dependencies added. Status dropdown behavior and aria-label preserved unchanged.
